note: IB Diploma Programme Group 6 — The Arts. Students may also take a second subject from Groups 1–5 in place of Group 6. Subjects: Visual Arts, Music, Theatre, Dance, Film. Each assessed by portfolio/process journal plus exhibition, performance, or project.

topics.subject: Visual Arts
topics.subtopicTitle: Analysis of artworks from different cultural contexts
topics.description: SL: analyse and compare at least 3 artworks from at least 2 different artists. HL: at least 4 artworks from at least 3 artists. Students investigate formal and conceptual qualities and the cultural contexts of artworks.

topics.subject: Visual Arts
topics.subtopicTitle: Development of artistic skills and ideas
topics.description: SL: 9–18 screens; HL: 13–25 screens. Documents the student's artistic development: experimentation, research, reflection, and skill-building across at least two art-making forms.

topics.subject: Visual Arts
topics.description: SL: 4–7 works; HL: 8–11 works. A coherent body of work with a curatorial rationale. Works are physically exhibited and assessed on concept, technical execution, and presentation.

topics.subject: Music
topics.description: Students listen to and analyse unfamiliar music from a range of times, places, and cultures. Develop musical perception and the ability to discuss musical elements, contexts, and cultural significance.

topics.subject: Music
topics.description: Students experiment with musical composition through a process journal. Develop and refine original music in at least two different musical roles (composition, performance, improvisation).

topics.subject: Music
topics.description: Students present music through a solo performance, media music composition, or creating a music product. Assessed on technical and expressive qualities relative to the chosen musical context.
